pn_naming_conventions: Long alphanumeric format: 2CDGxxxxRxxxx (13 chars, seems to be industrial/automation products)
Short alphanumeric format: CR-MH style (dash-separated, marketing materials/accessories)
Pattern 2CDG indicates likely building automation/KNX products
R in position 9-10 appears to be revision/variant indicator

ean_locations.primary: datasheet_parse - ABB datasheets reliably contain EAN codes

common_pitfalls: EXTREME price outlier: IPS/S3.1.1 shows 567,721.12 - likely data entry error or pack/project price, normal ABB DIN rail devices range 3-1000 EUR
Short PNs like 'CR-MH' may be catalogs/handbooks not actual products - verify if orderable item
EAN prefix 401677 is correct for ABB, other prefixes should be validated
Datasheet size >10MB (like 33MB for CR-MH) indicates documentation/catalog not product datasheet
Missing weight/dimensions often indicates non-physical product (software, documentation)

validation_rules.rule: if norm_price > 10000 then FLAG for review
validation_rules.reason: Standard ABB building automation components rarely exceed 5000 EUR; 567K price is obvious error

validation_rules.rule: if PN format is short (< 6 chars) AND specs_count = 0, classify as 'documentation'
validation_rules.reason: CR-MH pattern: short PN, 0 specs, huge PDF = handbook not product

validation_rules.rule: EAN must start with 40167 for ABB, otherwise require secondary verification
validation_rules.reason: ABB's registered GS1 prefix range

validation_rules.rule: if > 5000 KB (5MB), verify this is product not catalog
validation_rules.reason: 33MB 'datasheet' for CR-MH is actually full handbook

validation_rules.rule: if empty AND price > 50, require manual check
validation_rules.reason: Physical products should have dimensions; missing = incomplete data

notes_for_cheap_ai: ABB uses structured long PNs (2CDGxxxxRxxxx format) for real products. Always check official ABB sites first (new.abb.com, search.abb.com). EANs are reliably in datasheets - parse PDFs from library.e.abb.com. RED FLAG: prices above 10k are almost always errors (see example: 567k for a small DIN rail interface is impossible). Short PNs with zero specs are usually documentation not products. ABB building automation products typically weigh 50-500g and fit on DIN rails (MDRC = Modular DIN Rail Case). Trust RS Online and Siemens Mall for verification. When you see massive price anomalies, check if it's a pack quantity or currency error before accepting.

pn_naming_conventions: Формат XXXXX-RU или XXXXXX-RU (5-6 цифр + дефис + RU) — основной паттерн для российского рынка
Формат CMXXXXXX-RU (буквы CM + 6 цифр + дефис + RU) — серия металлорукавов и кабельных систем
Суффикс -RU означает русскую локализацию/спецификацию продукта

ean_locations.primary: gemini_grounding показывает EAN, но покрытие низкое (1 из 5). EAN формата 13 цифр с префиксом 4690309 (российский производитель)

common_pitfalls: КРИТИЧНО: Title и series часто содержат МУСОРНЫЕ данные (например '36144-RU' имеет title 'African Journal of Agricultural Research' — это явно ошибка парсинга)
Домены buildings.honeywell.com, resideo.com, honeywellhome.com НЕ относятся к DKC — это перекрёстное загрязнение данных из другого бренда
tme.eu, santehmoskva.ru могут показывать аналоги или неточные совпадения — проверять соответствие PN
Огромный разброс цен: от 74₽ (CM100600-RU метраж) до 42000₽ (36146-RU комплект) — обязательно проверять единицу измерения
us.rs-online.com, satro-paladin.com — низкая релевантность для российских PN с суффиксом -RU

validation_rules.rule: Если title не содержит слова на русском и не связан с электротехникой — отбросить, искать заново
validation_rules.reason: Видны примеры загрязнения: 'African Journal', 'QST Amateur Radio', 'DIXI Centre Drill' — это не DKC продукты

validation_rules.rule: Если price < 100₽ — проверить единицу измерения (может быть цена за метр). Если price > 20000₽ — проверить pack size или комплектацию
validation_rules.reason: CM100600-RU = 74₽/метр vs 36146-RU = 42000₽ за комплект

validation_rules.rule: EAN должен начинаться с 4690309. Если другой префикс — требуется дополнительная проверка
validation_rules.reason: DKC использует российский регистрационный код 4690309

validation_rules.rule: PN должен соответствовать паттерну *-RU. Если нет суффикса -RU — возможно это не для российского рынка
validation_rules.reason: Все примеры содержат -RU суффикс

validation_rules.rule: ИГНОРИРОВАТЬ данные с buildings.honeywell.com, resideo.com, honeywellhome.com для бренда DKC
validation_rules.reason: Это домены Honeywell — конкурентного бренда, данные загрязнены

notes_for_cheap_ai: DKC — российский производитель кабельных систем и электротехники. КРИТИЧНО: в обучающих данных есть СИЛЬНОЕ загрязнение — title содержат названия журналов и инструментов, URL ведут на Honeywell (другой бренд). При обогащении: 1) Используй ТОЛЬКО домены dkc.ru/dkcmarket.ru/elektro.ru для specs и EAN. 2) ИГНОРИРУЙ полностью домены honeywell/resideo. 3) Если title выглядит странно (журналы, сверла, не электротехника) — НЕ доверяй, ищи заново. 4) Цены проверяй на единицу измерения: есть и метровые товары (~70₽) и комплекты (~40000₽). 5) EAN начинается с 4690309, формат PN: цифры-RU или буквы+цифры-RU. 6) Datasheets есть (6-14 МБ), ищи на официальном сайте DKC.

pn_naming_conventions: 6-digit numeric format (e.g., 788600, 802371, 802374) - modern product line
Dot-notation format XX####.## (e.g., 010130.10, 027913.10, 033588.17) - older/modular systems
Short 5-digit numeric (e.g., 13960, 42511) - may indicate accessories, documentation, or cross-listed Honeywell products
No alphabetic prefixes observed in genuine Esser PNs

ean_locations.primary: NOT AVAILABLE in current dataset - EAN field empty across all samples
ean_locations.format: Unknown - requires scraping from esser-systems.com product pages or distributor sites
ean_locations.warning: Esser is B2B fire safety brand - EANs may only appear on distributor sites, not manufacturer pages

common_pitfalls: Honeywell cross-contamination: Esser is owned by Honeywell - domains like buildings.honeywell.com and security.honeywell.de appear but may show HVAC/security products unrelated to Esser fire detection (see PN 13960 'Axial Flow Valves' - likely wrong match)
eBay/generic marketplaces: Appear frequently but unreliable for specs/EAN (used items, unclear sourcing)
PEHA brand confusion: PN 42511 shows peha.de domain - likely incorrect cross-match with different manufacturer
High price variance: Prices range €24-€1134 - validate against product type (detectors ~€50-150, control modules €200-500, software €30-50)
Documentation vs. products: Some PNs may be manuals/software (e.g., 033588.17 'installation medium' at €33) not physical devices
Dot-notation version suffixes: .10, .17 likely indicate revisions - must match exactly

validation_rules.rule: If price >€400, verify it's control panel/base board, not detector
validation_rules.reason: Detectors typically €50-150, only control modules exceed €400 (e.g., 027913.10 MB-Secure PRO at €512)

validation_rules.rule: If price <€30, check if it's software/documentation not hardware
validation_rules.reason: PN 033588.17 'installation medium' €33, PN 802374 €24.64 - may be licenses/docs

validation_rules.rule: Reject if PN contains letters (except in series name like IQ8Quad)
validation_rules.reason: All valid Esser PNs are numeric or numeric with dots

validation_rules.rule: Flag peha.de, buildings.honeywell.com, new.abb.com as likely incorrect matches
validation_rules.reason: These brands found in dataset but don't align with Esser fire detection focus

validation_rules.rule: Validate title contains fire safety terms: detector, alarm, smoke, heat, transponder, control panel, isolator
validation_rules.reason: Titles like 'Axial Flow Valves' or 'Transistor' indicate wrong product match

validation_rules.rule: If series='IQ8Quad', price should be €80-150 range
validation_rules.reason: IQ8Quad is detector line with consistent pricing (PN 802371 at €108)

notes_for_cheap_ai: Esser is a German fire detection/alarm system manufacturer (owned by Honeywell). CRITICAL: Validate all Honeywell domains actually show Esser products - many false matches found with HVAC/building control products. The brand uses numeric-only part numbers in two formats: 6-digit (newer) and dot-notation (older modules). EANs are missing from manufacturer site - must scrape from distributors like brandmelde-shop.de. Price sanity check is essential: detectors €50-150, control boards €200-600, anything else is suspect. Ignore eBay for specs. Use esser-systems.com as ground truth for product existence and technical specs. Watch for software/documentation items priced under €50. If title doesn't mention fire/smoke/alarm/detector, it's probably a wrong match. Series names like IQ8Quad are reliable identifiers when present.

pn_naming_conventions: Numeric 10-digit format (e.g., 1061200000, 1631930000) - likely Weidmüller products misattributed to Honeywell
Alphanumeric with slashes (e.g., 129464N/U) - actual Honeywell controls
Dash-separated numeric (e.g., 203-183-420) - barcode/scanning equipment
Short alphanumeric codes (e.g., 240W, 304L) - possibly suffixes or model variants, high confusion risk
7-digit numeric (e.g., 3240197, 3240357) - industrial components, possibly Phoenix Contact misattribution

ean_locations.primary: gemini_grounding is most common source, distributor and datasheet_parse are more reliable when available
ean_locations.format: 13-digit EAN (400819..., 008526..., 083791..., 402551..., 404635...), leading zeros indicate UPC-A conversion

common_pitfalls: Brand misattribution: PNs starting with 10-digit, 16-digit numbers (1061200000, 1631930000) are Weidmüller, NOT Honeywell
Brand misattribution: 7-digit PNs like 3240197, 3240357 appear to be Phoenix Contact or similar
Generic PNs: '240W', '304L' are too generic - require series/context (AMD series, Bilsom series) for correct identification
Barcode equipment confusion: 203-xxx-xxx format is for scanning hardware, different product category with specialized suppliers
Price outliers: Prices range from €2 to €313, validate against product category (relays vs controls vs amplifiers)
Empty titles: PN 203-183-420 has no title, likely data quality issue
Missing EANs: Some products (304L, 3240357) have no EAN despite available datasheets

validation_rules.rule: If PN is 10-digit starting with 10/16/24/32, cross-check brand against Weidmüller/Phoenix Contact catalogs
validation_rules.reason: High rate of brand misattribution in dataset

validation_rules.rule: If PN is very short (2-4 chars) or common word (240W), require series/family validation
validation_rules.reason: Generic identifiers cause false matches

validation_rules.rule: If price >€100 and product is relay/switch, verify it's not a control system or amplifier
validation_rules.reason: Price range suggests different product categories: relays €2-3, controls €130-250, amplifiers €300+

validation_rules.rule: Prefer 'distributor' or 'datasheet_parse' sources over 'gemini_grounding'
validation_rules.reason: Direct sources more reliable than AI inference

validation_rules.rule: If title is empty or generic ('Microswitches, mechanical'), flag for manual review
validation_rules.reason: Indicates incomplete data scraping

validation_rules.rule: If barcodefactory.com or barcodesinc.com appear, verify product is scanning/printing equipment
validation_rules.reason: Domain clustering indicates product category

notes_for_cheap_ai: CRITICAL: This dataset has severe brand misattribution issues. Many products labeled Honeywell are actually Weidmüller (10-digit PNs starting 10/16), Phoenix Contact (7-digit starting 32), or other brands. Always verify brand before enriching. For true Honeywell products: focus on control systems (EC series), barcode scanners (203-xxx series), amplifiers (AMD series), and safety equipment (Bilsom series). Use DigiKey as primary validation source. When PN is ambiguous (like 240W, 304L), you MUST find the series/family name first. Gemini_grounding EANs need validation from actual distributor sites. Price sanity check: components €2-10, controls €50-250, specialized equipment €300+. If you see weidmueller.com or phoenixcontact.com in training URLs, that's the real manufacturer, not Honeywell.

pn_naming_conventions: 7-digit numeric format (1000000-1999999 range)
Pure numbers, no letters or special characters
Leading '10' prefix is common pattern
No model suffixes or variants in PN itself

ean_locations.primary: gemini_grounding and distributor sites (RS Online, Fabory) are most reliable
ean_locations.pattern: EAN often follows PN logic: 7312550[PN without first digit][check digit]

common_pitfalls: HIGH PRICES indicate dispensers/bulk packs (e.g., PN 1006186 at 1794.0 is dispenser, not single unit)
Missing titles or non-English titles (PN 1012541 has Russian title, 1012539 empty) - always verify on Honeywell official
Catalog PDFs (PN 1015021) being matched as products - exclude if title contains 'Catalog', 'Guide', 'Brochure'
eBay and marketplace sites show pack quantities in price - cross-reference with official distributors
Series names vary: 'Bilsom 300 Series', 'HL400-AM' - series is product line, not always present
EAN source 'datasheet_parse' can be unreliable if OCR errors - validate format 731255XXXXXXX

validation_rules.rule: if norm_price > 500, verify it's a dispenser or bulk pack (check title for 'DISPENSER', 'Box', '100pc')
validation_rules.reason: Individual ear protection items typically cost 10-100 EUR

validation_rules.rule: must start with 731255 or 731250, must be exactly 13 digits
validation_rules.reason: Honeywell Safety Products uses these prefixes consistently

validation_rules.rule: must be 7 digits, no spaces, no letters
validation_rules.reason: Howard Leight uses pure numeric codes in 10XXXXX range

validation_rules.rule: if empty or contains 'Catalog', 'Brochure', non-English gibberish - flag for manual review
validation_rules.reason: Indicates document match or bad scraping, not actual product

validation_rules.rule: if > 10000kb (10MB), likely full catalog not product sheet
validation_rules.reason: PN 1011994 has 27MB datasheet - probably entire product line catalog

validation_rules.rule: if 0 and datasheet exists, re-parse datasheet; if still 0, mark confidence low
validation_rules.reason: Multiple PNs have 0 specs despite large datasheets

notes_for_cheap_ai: Howard Leight is Honeywell's hearing protection brand. ALWAYS search official Honeywell domains first (sps-support.honeywell.com, automation.honeywell.com). Part numbers are exactly 7 digits. EANs always start 73125. CRITICAL: Check if high prices (>500) are for dispensers/bulk - look for keywords 'dispenser', 'refill station', 'box of'. If title is missing or looks wrong (non-English, says 'Catalog'), don't trust that source. RS Online (uk.rs-online.com) is gold standard for both EAN and pricing validation. When you find a datasheet, extract: NRR rating, cord/uncorded, reusable/disposable, material type. Series names like 'Bilsom' or 'HL400' indicate product family. Amazon/eBay prices often include multi-packs - divide by quantity. If you see 'AM' in model, it means antimicrobial. Validate every EAN by checking if middle digits loosely match the PN.

pn_naming_conventions: 6-digit numeric format (e.g., 020211, 101411, 106511)
PN often appears with leading zeros - store as 8 digits: 00020211
Inconsistent zero-padding: seen both '00020211' and '106511' formats
Some PNs may be mismatched - titles like 'CT40 Mobile Computer', 'Boiler Manual', 'Butterfly Valve' suggest cross-contamination from other brands

ean_locations.primary: gemini_grounding most common, datasheet_parse reliable for NOVA series
ean_locations.format: 13-digit EAN starting with 4010105 (PEHA prefix) + 6-digit PN + check digit (e.g., 4010105020212 for PN 00020211)
ean_locations.construction_rule: EAN = 4010105 + [6-digit PN without leading zeros] + [check digit]
ean_locations.construction_rule_VERIFIED: EAN = 4010105 + first_5_digits_of_PN(zero-padded) + check_digit. Verified on 7/8 known EANs (87.5% match).
ean_locations.python_formula: def predict_ean(pn): pn_5=pn.lstrip("0").zfill(6)[:5]; first_12="4010105"+pn_5; return first_12 + calc_ean13_check(first_12)

common_pitfalls: CRITICAL: Data contamination - PNs 104011 (CT40 Mobile Computer/Honeywell), 127411 (V51E Butterfly Valve), 155111 (Boiler Manual), 157413 (OWNER'S MANUAL) are clearly NOT PEHA products
Price range suspicious: 4.77 to 150.75 EUR suggests mixed product categories or pack pricing
Empty titles and zero specs (5 out of 8 records) indicate poor data quality
Large datasheet sizes (15891 KB, 26499 KB) for items with no specs suggests PDF parsing failures or wrong document matches
eBay.co.uk as source is unreliable for specs/official data
electrica-online.ru appears twice but may not be authoritative for German brand PEHA

validation_rules.rule: Must start with 4010105, be exactly 13 digits, and match PN pattern
validation_rules.reason: PEHA has consistent GS1 prefix, can construct EAN from PN for validation

validation_rules.rule: Should be 6-8 digit numeric only, reject if title contains unrelated product types
validation_rules.reason: Seen contamination with Honeywell scanners, valves, boilers - validate title matches electrical installation products

validation_rules.rule: If empty or contains 'Manual', 'Butterfly Valve', 'Mobile Computer', 'Boiler' - flag for review
validation_rules.reason: PEHA manufactures switches, sockets, frames - these titles indicate wrong product match

validation_rules.rule: If >100 EUR for basic PN (without 'set' in title), verify not bulk pack
validation_rules.reason: PN 104011 at 150.75 EUR likely wrong product or pack price

validation_rules.rule: If >5000 KB but specs_count=0, PDF parsing failed or wrong document
validation_rules.reason: PNs 155111, 157413 have huge PDFs but zero specs extracted

validation_rules.rule: PEHA products typically have series (NOVA, Standard, etc.) - if empty, data may be incomplete
validation_rules.reason: Only 1 of 8 has series populated, suggests missing enrichment

search_query_templates: 4010105{pn_6digit} (direct EAN search if PN is 6 digits)
notes_for_cheap_ai: PEHA is a German manufacturer of electrical installation equipment (switches, sockets, frames). CRITICAL DATA QUALITY ISSUE: This dataset contains significant contamination - at least 50% of samples are NOT PEHA products (Honeywell scanners, industrial valves, boilers). Before enriching: 1) Validate title matches PEHA product categories (Schalter/switches, Steckdosen/sockets, Rahmen/frames, Dimmer, Taster/buttons). 2) Official source is peha.de - prioritize this for all data. 3) EAN follows strict pattern: 4010105 + 6-digit PN + check digit. 4) PN is typically 6 digits, sometimes zero-padded to 8. 5) Ignore data from eBay, electrica-online.ru for specs. 6) If title is empty or unrelated to electrical installation, mark record as 'needs_validation' rather than enriching with wrong data. 7) Price range for individual items typically 5-70 EUR; outliers likely indicate wrong matches. 8) Always cross-check: does the title make sense for an electrical components manufacturer?
